desc: Hell-Ville is Nosy Be's ferry-and-flight gateway, balancing Creole market culture with access to reefs, forest reserves, and a beach-paced island rhythm that works best in the drier trade-wind season.
hls: Main launch point for Nosy Tanikely, Nosy Komba, and nearby islets
Strong dry-season snorkeling/diving visibility and calmer sea windows
Spice-island markets, seafood, and Malagasy-Creole street life
Seasonal whale-shark and humpback excursion access

todo.name: Hell-Ville bazary be and harbor walk
todo.desc: Explore spice stalls, colonial facades, and harbor traffic early before midday heat.

todo.name: Nosy Tanikely marine reserve snorkel
todo.desc: Join a guided day boat for coral gardens, reef fish, and reserve beach stops.

todo.name: Lokobe Reserve pirogue and forest walk
todo.desc: Cross by pirogue for endemic lemurs, chameleons, and humid forest trails.

todo.name: Nosy Komba village and black-lemur stop
todo.desc: Combine craft villages with guided forest sections for wildlife sightings.

todo.name: Mont Passot sunset circuit
todo.desc: Time a late-afternoon climb for crater-lake panoramas and sunset viewpoints.

todo.name: Whale-shark excursion (seasonal)
todo.desc: Best odds are usually in autumn shoulder months when operators run targeted trips.

wifi.note: Usable in town hotels; island-wide outages and power cuts are common.

prac.fltNote: From the UK/Ireland, common routings are one-stop to Antananarivo (TNR) via hubs like Addis Ababa, Nairobi, Istanbul, or Paris, then a domestic connection to Nosy Be (NOS); some seasons also have limited direct/regional charter access to NOS, while Antsiranana (DIE) can work as a northern backup with onward surface/boat logistics.
prac.currency: Madagascar local currency (check live rates before booking).
prac.lang: Local language plus tourism-level English in main visitor areas.
prac.tz: Check local timezone and DST rules for exact travel dates.
prac.visa: Confirm current entry/transit rules for your passport before booking.
alerts: Cyclone-season weather (roughly Jan-Mar) can disrupt domestic flights, ferries, and small-boat excursions with little notice.
Inter-island boats and marine trips depend on sea state and port conditions; keep buffer days before international departures.
Road transfers between northern lodges and Hell-Ville can become slow after heavy rain, and evening travel times are often longer than map estimates.

sweet: Jun-Oct for the best blend of dry weather, clearer water, and manageable humidity, with Sep-Nov strongest for marine-wildlife trips but higher flight/lodge pressure.
